tasks.title: Extend Post access schemas with relation fields
tasks.description:
Extend PostPublicSchema, PostProtectedSchema, and PostAdminSchema in `packages/schemas/src/entities/post/post.access.schema.ts` to include 5 relation fields: `author` (UserPublicSchema), `relatedAccommodation` (AccommodationPublicSchema), `relatedDestination` (DestinationPublicSchema), `relatedEvent` (EventPublicSchema), and `sponsorship` (PostSponsorshipPublicSchema.extend({ sponsor: UserPublicSchema.optional() })). All relation fields must be `.optional()`. Each tier uses tier-appropriate schemas (Public→*PublicSchema, Protected→*ProtectedSchema, Admin→*AdminSchema). Watch for circular references between Post↔User — use getter properties if needed (Zod v4 preferred pattern). The `import type { z }` must become `import { z }` to use `.extend()`. Run boundary tests after changes.

Files: `packages/schemas/src/entities/post/post.access.schema.ts`
Tests: Run `pnpm test -- packages/schemas/test/entities/access-boundary/access-boundary.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Import relation schemas (User, Accommodation, Destination, Event, PostSponsorship)
Extend PublicSchema with 5 relation fields using *PublicSchema
Extend ProtectedSchema with 5 relation fields using *ProtectedSchema
Extend AdminSchema with 5 relation fields using *AdminSchema
Handle nested sponsorship→sponsor at each tier level
Check for circular references (Post↔User)
Run boundary tests

tasks.title: Extend Accommodation access schemas with destination relation
tasks.description:
Extend AccommodationPublicSchema, AccommodationProtectedSchema, and AccommodationAdminSchema in `packages/schemas/src/entities/accommodation/accommodation.access.schema.ts` to include `destination` relation field. Public tier already has `owner`, `amenities`, `features`, `faqs` via `.extend()`. Only `destination: DestinationPublicSchema.optional()` is missing. Protected tier needs `destination` added too. Verify dynamic relations (`amenities`, `features` arrays) already exist. Each tier uses tier-appropriate Destination schema.

Files: `packages/schemas/src/entities/accommodation/accommodation.access.schema.ts`
Tests: Run boundary tests after changes.

subtasks.title: Import DestinationPublicSchema, DestinationProtectedSchema, DestinationAdminSchema
Add destination relation to PublicSchema .extend()
Add destination relation to ProtectedSchema
Add destination relation to AdminSchema
Verify amenities/features arrays already present
Run boundary tests

tasks.title: Extend DestinationReview access schemas with relation fields and preemptive lifecycleState

Extend DestinationReviewPublicSchema, DestinationReviewProtectedSchema, and DestinationReviewAdminSchema in `packages/schemas/src/entities/destinationReview/destinationReview.access.schema.ts` to include `user` and `destination` relation fields. All `.optional()`. Additionally, preemptively add `lifecycleState: z.nativeEnum(LifecycleStatusEnum).optional()` to AdminSchema ONLY (for forward compatibility with SPEC-063). Do NOT add lifecycleState to Public or Protected schemas. Change `import type { z }` to `import { z }`.

Tests: Run boundary tests after changes.

tasks.title: Extend Sponsorship access schemas with relation fields and preemptive lifecycleState
tasks.description:
Extend SponsorshipPublicSchema, SponsorshipProtectedSchema, and SponsorshipAdminSchema in `packages/schemas/src/entities/sponsorship/sponsorship.access.schema.ts` to include `sponsorUser` (User*Schema), `level`, and `package` relation fields. Tests: Run boundary tests after changes.

tasks.title: Enhance boundary tests with explicit sensitive field absence assertions
tasks.description:
Update `packages/schemas/test/entities/access-boundary/access-boundary.test.ts` to add explicit absence assertions for ALL sensitive fields from the 'Sensitive Fields at Risk' table: `createdById`, `updatedById`, `deletedById`, `deletedAt`, `adminInfo`, `notes`, `lifecycleState`, `paymentId`. For each of the 23 schema sets, verify that PublicSchema strips admin fields, ProtectedSchema strips admin-only fields, and AdminSchema retains all fields. Also verify that the newly added relation fields from T-001 through T-009 are correctly handled (present when data provided, absent when not). Ensure test uses `expect(parsed.fieldName).toBeUndefined()` pattern for absence.

Files: `packages/schemas/test/entities/access-boundary/access-boundary.test.ts`
Tests: Run `pnpm test -- packages/schemas/test/entities/access-boundary/`

subtasks.title: Add createdById/updatedById/deletedById/deletedAt absence assertions to all 23 Public schemas
Add adminInfo absence assertions to 15 applicable Public schemas
Add notes absence assertion to Tag Public schema
Add lifecycleState absence assertion to applicable Public schemas
Add paymentId absence assertion to Sponsorship Public schema
Add Protected tier admin-field absence assertions
Verify AdminSchema retains all fields including new relations
Run all boundary tests

tasks.title: Run full typecheck and test suite for Phase 0 verification
tasks.description:
Run `pnpm typecheck && pnpm test` to verify all Phase 0 changes pass. Fix any type errors or test failures. This is the final quality gate before Phase 0 can be merged. After this task, Phase 0 is complete. Do NOT proceed to Phase 1 — it is blocked until SPEC-063 and SPEC-066 are merged.

Commands: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm test`

subtasks.title: Run pnpm typecheck
Run pnpm test
Fix any failures

tasks.title: Add stripWithSchema utility function to response-helpers.ts
tasks.description:
Add a `stripWithSchema<T>()` helper function to `apps/api/src/utils/response-helpers.ts`. The function accepts `data: T` and `responseSchema?: ZodType`, calls `responseSchema.safeParse(data)` when schema is provided, returns stripped data on success, logs a warning and returns unstripped data on failure. Use `@repo/logger` for structured warning log. Export the function for testing.

Files: `apps/api/src/utils/response-helpers.ts`
Tests: Unit test in `apps/api/test/utils/response-helpers.test.ts` — test strip success, strip no-op (no schema), strip fallback (parse failure).

subtasks.title: Implement stripWithSchema function
Add structured warning log on safeParse failure
Write unit tests for all 3 paths

tasks.title: Modify createResponse to accept and apply responseSchema
tasks.description: Add optional `responseSchema?: ZodType` parameter to `createResponse()` in `apps/api/src/utils/response-helpers.ts`. Call `stripWithSchema(data, responseSchema)` before wrapping data in the response envelope. Backward compatible: routes without responseSchema continue unchanged. Add JSDoc documenting the new parameter.

subtasks.title: Add responseSchema parameter to createResponse signature
Call stripWithSchema before envelope wrapping
Add JSDoc
Verify backward compatibility

tasks.title: Modify createPaginatedResponse to accept and apply responseSchema per item
tasks.description: Add optional `responseSchema?: ZodType` parameter to `createPaginatedResponse()`. Apply `stripWithSchema()` to each item in the `items[]` array. Pagination metadata is NOT subject to stripping. Add JSDoc documenting that schema stripping applies to items only.

subtasks.title: Add responseSchema parameter to createPaginatedResponse signature
Apply stripWithSchema to each item in items array
Add JSDoc noting stripping applies to items only
Test with populated items, empty items, and parse failure

tasks.description: Modify route factory functions in `apps/api/src/utils/route-factory.ts` to pass `options.responseSchema` to `createResponse()` and `createPaginatedResponse()` calls. Tiered factories propagate automatically via spread.

subtasks.title: Pass options.responseSchema to createResponse in createSimpleRoute

tasks.title: Write integration test for safeParse fallback path
tasks.description: Verify the safeParse fallback: when the service returns data that fails schema validation, the route still responds 200 with the unstripped payload and a structured warning is logged. Test in `apps/api/test/schema-validation/field-enforcement/fallback.test.ts`.

tasks.title: Run full test suite, fix regressions, and create route inventory
tasks.description: Run `pnpm test` across the API to detect regressions from Phase 1 changes. Result: 2761 pass, 4 pre-existing failures in `admin-list-routes.test.ts` for AccommodationReview/DestinationReview (403 vs 200 — Hono sibling-route middleware collision, documented in project memory, unrelated to SPEC-062 stripping). New schema-stripping tests (unit + integration-style) all pass.
